On this page

What is Computing? ​

Your phone, your laptop, the traffic light at the corner — they all process information. Before you can understand how they do it, you need to understand what a computer actually is.

Definitions ​

TermDefinition
IT (Information Technology)The use of computers and software to manage, store, process and communicate information
ICT (Information & Communication Technology)IT plus communication technology — combining computing with networks and the internet
ComputerAn electronic device that accepts data as input, processes it, stores results and produces output
DataRaw, unprocessed facts and figures (e.g. the number 37)
InformationData that has been processed and given meaning (e.g. "the temperature is 37°C")
KnowledgeApplying information to make decisions (e.g. "37°C is a fever — see a doctor")

Grade 10 — Foundation

This entire page is core Grade 10 content. Know all definitions and the computer model cold.

The General Computer Model ​

Every computer — from a microcontroller to a high-end workstation — follows the same basic model:

ComponentRoleExamples
InputGets data into the computerKeyboard, mouse, microphone, scanner, webcam, touchscreen
ProcessManipulates and transforms dataCPU (Central Processing Unit)
OutputPresents results to the userMonitor, printer, speaker, projector
StorageKeeps data short-term or long-termRAM (short-term), HDD/SSD/USB (long-term)
CommunicationSends/receives data to/from other devicesNetwork card, modem, Wi-Fi adapter

Exam Tip

The acronym IPOSC (Input → Process → Output → Storage → Communication) helps you remember the model. Most exam questions give you a device and ask which component role it plays.

Hardware vs Software ​

HardwareSoftware
What it isPhysical components you can touchPrograms and instructions the computer runs
ExamplesCPU, RAM, keyboard, monitorWindows, Delphi, MS Word
TypesInput, output, storage, processing, communicationSystem software, application software
Can be changed?Must be physically replacedInstalled, updated, deleted

Data vs Information vs Knowledge ​

LevelWhat it isExample
DataRaw, unprocessed facts37, John, Cape Town
InformationData processed and given contextJohn's temperature is 37°C
KnowledgeUsing information to make decisions37°C is a fever — John needs to rest and see a doctor

Advantages of Using Computers ​

  1. Provides access to more information
  2. Completes tasks that might be impossible for humans (e.g. weather modelling, space calculations)
  3. Saves time
  4. Automates repetitive tasks
  5. Allows for greater productivity
  6. Allows for better communication and connections
  7. Entertainment

Disadvantages of Using Computers ​

DisadvantageExplanation
Social risksSocial media can be addictive, cause jealousy, and interfere with real friendships
Health risksExcessive use causes back pain, eyestrain, obesity, RSI, and CTS
Security risksMalware can attack systems, destroy files, steal data or allow unauthorised access
High costEven affordable computers are expensive for many South Africans
DistractionsHigh entertainment value makes it easy to lose focus and productivity
Environmental impactComputers consume large amounts of electricity; e-waste is a growing problem

Key Terms

RSI (Repetitive Strain Injury) — painful inflammation of tendons caused by overuse (e.g. excessive typing or mouse use)
CTS (Carpal Tunnel Syndrome) — compression of the nerve in the wrist from repetitive hand/wrist movements
Ergonomics — the study of how humans interact with manufactured objects, aiming to create environments suited to users' physical needs

Key Terms ​

TermDefinition
DataRaw, unprocessed facts
InformationProcessed data with meaning
KnowledgeInformation applied to make decisions
HardwarePhysical components of a computer
SoftwarePrograms and instructions
ITInformation Technology
ICTInformation and Communication Technology
CPUCentral Processing Unit — the processor
Input deviceGets data into the computer
Output devicePresents results to the user
RSIRepetitive Strain Injury
ErgonomicsDesigning equipment for human comfort and efficiency

Exam Focus — Paper 2 ​

  • Define IT, ICT, data, information, knowledge
  • Explain the computer model (IPOSC) and give examples of each component
  • Classify a given device: input / output / storage / processing / communication
  • Distinguish between hardware and software with examples
  • Give TWO advantages and TWO disadvantages of computers
  • Define RSI and name ONE way to prevent it
